latest.title: Net Asset Value(s)
latest.content:
Today 10:41
RNS Number : 4277D
JPMorgan Emerging EMEA Securities
07 May 2026
JPMORGAN EMERGING EUROPE, MIDDLE EAST & AFRICA SECURITIES PLC (the 'Company')
Legal Entity Identifier: 549300II3MHI98ZLVH37
THE COMPANY ANNOUNCES THE UNAUDITED NET ASSET VALUE (NAV) As at: 06 May 2026
THE NET ASSET VALUE PER SHARE IN PENCE, INCLUDING INCOME WITH DEBT AT FAIR VALUE:
68.74
The Directors consider that in the absence of observable market data on its Russian investments resulting from the closure of the Moscow Exchange (MOEX) to overseas investors, there has been a material change to the market value of its Russian investments and therefore a fair value valuation methodology has been applied to those investments held. For its MOEX local stock, an alternative fair value adjustment has been applied to the last trade price on 25th February 2022. The price of these stocks has been determined by taking the live market price as at 25th February 2022 and applying a 99% provision for valuation. Similarly, for the American Depositary Receipts and Global Depositary Receipts an alternative fair value adjustment has been applied to the last trade price on 2nd March 2022 and a 99% provision for valuation applied.
Name of contact and telephone number for queries:
Paul Ainger 0044 207 742 6524
Name of authorised company official responsible for making this notification:
Paul Winship 0044 207 742 9815 - Company Secretary
Date: 07 May 2026

latest.title: Closed Period Notification
latest.content:
1 May 2026 14:50
RNS Number : 8652C
JPMorgan Emerging EMEA Securities
01 May 2026
LONDON STOCK EXCHANGE
ANNOUNCEMENT
JPMORGAN EMERGING EUROPE, MIDDLE EAST & AFRICA SECURITIES PLC
(the 'Company') Closed Period Notification
Legal Entity Identifier:
549300II3MHI98ZLVH37
Information disclosed in accordance with UK Market Abuse Regulation ('MAR') Article 19, paragraph 11
The Company announces that it is satisfied that any inside information which the Directors and the Company may have in the period leading up to the announcement of its results for the half year ended 30th April 2026 has previously been, and will continue to be, notified to the London Stock Exchange ('LSE') via a regulatory information service. Therefore, the Company is not prohibited from dealing in its own securities.
The mandatory closed period under MAR will commence on 11th May 2026 and will expire when the results in respect of the half year ended 30th April 2026 are published, which is expected to be on or around 22nd June 2026.
If in the period leading up to the announcement of the half year results the Directors of the Company come into the possession of any inside information, this will be notified to a RIS (the London Stock Exchange) without delay and before any such transactions are
undertaken.
JPMorgan Funds Limited - Company Secretary
1st May 2026
For further information, please
contact:
Joel Clopon
JPMorgan Funds Limited 0800 20 40 20

company_30s.what_they_do: JEMA is a UK‑registered investment vehicle that manages a diversified portfolio of assets, largely in Russia and Eastern Europe. The firm specialises in buying distressed or undervalued securities, restructuring them, and eventually selling for a profit.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company taps into a vast, largely untapped market of Russian assets that are still being re‑valued after sanctions. Its expertise in navigating complex legal and geopolitical environments gives it a competitive edge, making it a key player for investors looking for high‑risk, high‑reward opportunities in that region.
company_30s.current_state: JEMA is operating as a typical asset‑management entity, earning fees from its portfolio. Recent filings show a series of legal challenges and NAV volatility, suggesting the firm is under pressure from sanctions and court rulings. The share price is at a very low level, reflecting a deep market sell‑off.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy updating investors on its net asset value, announcing the end of a trading period, and detailing its largest holdings. A series of court case updates indicate ongoing legal battles linked to Russian sanctions, while a recent AGM report confirms management’s stance on governance and future plans.

Three clear themes emerge: (1) **Financial transparency** – regular NAV and period‑end disclosures; (2) **Legal & regulatory pressure** – court cases and sanction‑related updates; (3) **Strategic positioning** – a focus on large, high‑potential holdings and a potential shift in investment focus as sanctions ease.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but watchful – the community is largely silent, with a few cautious voices noting potential upside if sanctions ease.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of insiders point to a stable NAV and the possibility of new deals that could lift the company’s value. They see the current price as a buying opportunity.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Most traders fear the legal exposure and the uncertainty surrounding Russian sanctions. They worry that the company could be forced to liquidate assets at a loss or face further regulatory scrutiny.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has shifted from neutral to slightly bullish after a few posts about NAV stability and potential new holdings, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for JEMA is flat – no recent search spikes. This suggests the market is unaware, which is a positive sign for a contrarian play.

near_term_catalysts: The AGM results could signal a change in strategy or a new investment direction, which would be a clear signal for a bounce.
A resolution of the Russian court cases or a settlement of sanctions would remove a major drag on the business.
Any announcement of a new high‑profile holding or partnership could lift sentiment.
A significant spike in trading volume – e.g., 20‑30% above the 20‑day average – would indicate renewed interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden increase in volume, a clear statement from management about sanctions relief, or a fresh NAV that shows a marked improvement. A sudden uptick in media coverage about a new deal or settlement would also be a strong confirmation.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share is still in the early‑stage of a crash‑recovery cycle. Capitulation appears complete – the price has fallen far below recent highs and trading volume is low. A mean‑reversion bounce would likely require a catalyst that reduces the legal or sanction risk.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is down more than 70 % from its peak, which is the sweet spot for the highest‑return signal colour. Historically, such deep crashes have returned 160 % or more when a catalyst triggers a rebound. If the price climbs back to the midpoint of its historic range, the upside is roughly double the current level. The recent silence and flat search trend mean the market hasn’t priced in any upside yet.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Sanctions and court cases are still unresolved. If the legal battles worsen or the company is forced to liquidate assets at a loss, the price could stay depressed or fall further. The worst‑case scenario is a continued decline to a new trough, where the share could become essentially worthless.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 70 % of the current price (the depth of the crash). Potential gain: 100 %–200 % if the share recovers to the 50 %–75 % level of its historical high. The asymmetry favours a long position if a catalyst materialises.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when volume spikes 25 % above the 20‑day average and the price breaks a key support level, indicating capitulation is finished. Allocate 10 % of discretionary capital. Place a stop 15 % below the entry to limit the 70 % downside risk.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see one of the catalysts listed above – a fresh NAV, a court ruling, or a new holding announcement. Watch the 20‑day moving average; a breakout above it could be a buying signal.
playbook.if_passing: Only consider abandoning the position if the company announces bankruptcy, a delisting notice, or a major escalation in sanctions that forces asset sales.

ai_insights.bottom_line: JEMA is a deep‑crashed, low‑sentiment stock that could double its value once sanctions or legal issues resolve – but only if a clear catalyst removes the current drag.
